content.summaryText: Pediatric Fever Triage is part of Common Pediatric Illnesses in the theme Maternal and Child Health. This module focuses on: Use age-specific fever thresholds.

content.questionText: Which action best supports this course objective: Use age-specific fever thresholds?
content.explanation: The best answer is the action that directly helps learners apply 'Use age-specific fever thresholds' while studying Pediatric Fever Triage.

options.text: Use a structured checklist and verify outcomes
Skip baseline assessment to save time
Rely only on memory without documentation

content.summaryText: Practical emphasis: Identify serious bacterial risk. Learners should practice concise decision-making and escalation when necessary.

content.questionText: A clear plan for 'Identify serious bacterial risk' improves consistency in Pediatric Fever Triage.
content.explanation: Structured plans reduce omissions and improve safe, repeatable care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
